title: 10 possible side effects of Cimicifuga racemosa (Black Cohosh) if used improperly
intro: Cimicifuga racemosa, also known as black cohosh, is a plant commonly used in herbal medicine, particularly for menopausal symptoms. However, if used improperly, it can have several potential health side effects. First, it can cause stomach discomfort, including cramps, nausea, and black stools. Second, it may lead to headaches and dizziness, which can affect a person's ability to perform daily tasks. Third, it can cause rashes and other skin irritations, especially with topical use. Fourth, in some cases, it has been linked to a decrease in blood pressure, which can be dangerous for individuals with low blood pressure or those taking medication that affects blood pressure. Lastly, long-term use or overdose can result in liver damage, making it essential to use black cohosh under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
